accept: External reviewer (not Andrew) reads docs/orchestration-design.md at a pinned commit and reports findings

Reviewer prompt follows act skill discipline: pin commit hash, include changed file paths, require 'I read commit X at paths Y' as first line, >70% confidence floor, 'what's working well' closing section
Review findings either resolved inline (small doc edits) or filed as derivative act issues
Close with reason naming the reviewer(s) used and any derivative issue IDs filed
payload.description:
The 2026-05-11 session extended docs/orchestration-design.md with three substantive additions:

1. New "Orchestrator implementations" section that formalizes orchestrator as a role (not a tool) with three named implementations: Claude Code with sub-agents as the Mode B reference implementation available today; external harness (gas town on beads, or any equivalent) as the cross-runtime case; future built-in act-orchestrator as the eventual collapse-into-binary path. Explicit framing that the Claude Code auto-mode classifier is a CC-side constraint that the design accommodates by routing integration through the orchestrator, with the side benefit that any other implementation gets the same cleanliness for free.

2. Explicit worker/orchestrator push-asymmetry: in Mode B, the worker pushes to its assigned branch, not to main. The orchestrator handles integration to main on its own cadence. Mode A is recast as the degenerate case where worker and orchestrator collapse into the same session.

3. New "Log noise as a control surface" section that walks the design space from worker-driven op-per-commit (current Mode A canonical) through per_session bundling (existing act lever) through orchestrator-batched claims (Mode B reduces dedup distribution → fewer commits) through orchestrator-batched everything. Frames log volume as orchestrator policy, not act policy.

Plus: a "Do now" item to update the act skill with the push-asymmetry framing, and an open question on multi-op-per-write affordance for orchestrator-batched ops.

These additions reframe Mode A's current canonical loop as the degenerate case of Mode B and motivate a near-term skill update. Before any skill change or act surface change lands, get external review on whether the abstractions hold and whether the contract is implementable across the three named implementations.
payload.title: Review orchestration-design.md additions (orchestrator-as-role, push asymmetry, log noise control surface)
